body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
irrlicht_observes: yes

## Reasoning

The scenario asks whether aider can fail a model call mid-turn with no normal turn-end signal, and whether irrlicht returns the session to `ready` rather than leaving it stuck in `working`.

**agent_supports: yes.** Aider routes every model call through litellm, and a provider failure (network error, 5xx, timeout, bad request) surfaces as an error/exception that aider prints to the chat and appends to its per-project history file `.aider.chat.history.md` as a blockquote line — e.g. `> litellm.BadRequestError: OpenAIException - …`. No `> Tokens: …` accounting line is emitted in this case, so there is no normal end-of-call marker; the turn simply aborts. This is the canonical Bucket-A shape from the scenario description (transcript epilogue carrying synthesized error text).

**irrlicht_observes: yes.** The aider adapter is `Source: FilesUnderCWD` over the markdown history file, parsed by a `RawParser` (`core/adapters/inbound/agents/aider/parser.go`). The parser's `flushErrorTurn` matches the error blockquote via `errorRE` (`^>\s*\S*(?:Error|Exception)(?:[: ]|$)`), gated on `turnOpen`, and emits a synthetic `turn_done` carrying the error text as `AssistantText`. The state classifier maps a `turn_done` with no trailing question to `ready` (`state_classifier_test.go`: "working → ready (turn_done, no question)"). So the user-observable arc `ready → working → ready` holds: the session does not stick in `working`. This is the #262 fix, pinned by `TestParser_LLMError_EndsTurn` (synthetic turn_done with error text) and `TestParser_ErrorTurn_RecoversOnNextPrompt` (a subsequent `####` prompt reopens a fresh turn). This ratifies the existing matrix cell (`yes/yes`, note "#262 fixed — error blockquote emits turn_done early").

**Defense in depth via IdleFlush.** Even setting the error blockquote aside, aider's clean turn-end is synthesized by `IdleFlush` after `aiderIdleTurnDoneAfter` (1500ms) of transcript quiet, and the SessionDetector re-polls working sessions every ~5s. So any turn that opened (`####` seen) and then went quiet — including an aborted one — is flushed to `turn_done` → `ready` within roughly one sweep interval, exactly the primitive the scenario names ("sweep-based stall detection").

## What irrlicht does NOT see (and why it does not change the verdict)

The scenario splits failures into three buckets. Bucket A (error epilogue in the transcript) is the observable path covered above. Bucket B — a pure mid-stream network failure where aider writes NOTHING further to the markdown file and the turn never even opened a visible line — is the gap: aider has no working-state inactivity timer of its own that lands in the transcript, and PR #106 removed the daemon's stale-tool timer. In that narrow case the parser has no error line to match. In practice the turn has almost always already opened on a `####` user-prompt line, so `IdleFlush` still fires and resolves the session; the truly-silent variant is the residual caveat. The canonical spec asserts only the user-observable arc (`ready → working → ready`, final `ready`), which the Bucket-A + IdleFlush paths satisfy, so the verdict stays `yes` with the gap recorded as a caveat.

## Capability anchor

Primitive exercised: sweep-based stall detection / error-epilogue classification. The relevant capability flag in `replaydata/agents/aider/capabilities.json` is `headless_mode: true` (the scenario's declared `requires`); aider also exposes `streaming_output: true`, which is what makes a mid-stream provider failure reachable.
caveats: Bucket B (pure mid-stream network failure that writes no further line to `.aider.chat.history.md` and never opens a `####` turn) is unobservable: there is no working-state inactivity sweep landing in the transcript since PR #106 removed the stale-tool timer, and no error blockquote for the parser to match. In practice the turn has usually already opened, so IdleFlush still resolves it; the truly-silent variant is the residual gap.

The error blockquote must match `errorRE` (`Error`/`Exception` token after `>`). A provider failure surfaced under a different word (e.g. a bare `> Timed out` with no `Error`/`Exception` token) would not trigger flushErrorTurn directly and would instead rely on the slower IdleFlush path to reach `ready`.

sources.note: flushErrorTurn + errorRE emit synthetic turn_done on an LLM error blockquote (no `> Tokens:` line); IdleFlush synthesizes turn_done after 1500ms quiet
TestParser_LLMError_EndsTurn (#262) and TestParser_ErrorTurn_RecoversOnNextPrompt pin the abort + recovery paths
'working → ready (turn_done, no question)' confirms turn_done with error text resolves the session to ready
Source: FilesUnderCWD over .aider.chat.history.md via RawLineParser — transport that carries the error epilogue
headless_mode: true (scenario's requires) and streaming_output: true make a mid-turn provider failure reachable
current cell: yes/yes, note '#262 fixed — error blockquote emits turn_done early' — this assessment ratifies it
